text.title: Array Items Wrapper Core Rules
text.summary: Three testable rules every variable-cardinality extraction enforces.

rule.statement: Top-level structured-output schema is an object, not a bare list. The shape is `{items: list[Entity], total_found: int, truncated: bool}`.

rationale.source: OpenAI strict-mode docs; Azure SO requirements 2026

rule.rationale: Strict-mode rejects top-level arrays; lenient providers collapse zero/one/many into inconsistent shapes.

rule.statement: total_found reflects what the model believes the source contains, not the length of `items`. truncated=true iff total_found > len(items).
rule.rationale: Mismatched count signals lossy extraction and lets downstream decide to re-run with higher max_items.

rule.statement: Zero entities returned as `{items: [], total_found: 0, truncated: false}`. Never null and never a missing field.
rule.rationale: Null vs [] vs missing key is the #1 cause of crashed extraction parsers across providers.

rule.statement: Stub rule for conclusion 'r1' referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml. Replace with the real testable rule that resolves this branch of the tree.
rule.rationale: Auto-added by fix-methodology-phase-d.py to keep tree refs resolvable.

example.title: Bare array
example.note: Strict-mode rejects; parser must handle missing diagnostics.
